title: cyrtanthus sanguineus
intro: Cyrtanthus sanguineus, also known as blood-breath or blood hyacinth, is a flowering plant species native to the high-altitude grasslands and rocky outcrops of southern Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in alkaloids and other bioactive compounds that have been found to have health benefits, including antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. In herbal medicine, Cyrtanthus sanguineus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used as a tincture or dried extract to harness its medicinal properties.
